New perplexity computer plus comment update is insane AI can now control your entire browser. Not just search stuff, not just answer your questions. It can actually use websites for you. Log into your apps, click buttons, fill out forms, complete full tasks. And today I'm going to show you exactly how this brand new perplexity update works, why it changes everything, and how you can use it right now to get more done in less time. This is big. This is new. And honestly, this is a little bit scary. Let's get into it. So, here's what just happened. Perplexity just connected two of their biggest systems together. The first one is called Plexity computer. The second one is their AI browser called Comet. And when you put them together, something wild happens. The AI can now fully control your web browser, like a real person sitting at your desk. It opens sites. It clicks things. It fills in forms. It uses your logged in accounts. And it does all of this on its own. Think about that for a second. Before this update, AI could answer your questions. It could give you suggestions, but you still had to do the work. You still had to open the tab, click the link, copy the text, paste it somewhere, do all the boring stuff yourself. Now, you just tell it what to do. And it does it inside the browser. No extra tools, no complicated setup. Just type a prompt and watch it work. Let me break down the two pieces so this makes sense. First, Plexity Computer. This is basically a digital worker. is an AI agent that can control apps, automate tasks, browse websites, fill forms, send emails, do research, and handle multi-step workflows. It uses multiple AI models working together behind the scenes. Think of it like having an assistant who never sleeps and never complains. Second, Comet. This is Perplexity's own web browser, but it's not like Chrome or Safari. It has AI built right into it. So, it can summarize pages, research topics, manage your tabs, and actually do things on websites, not just show you information. It takes action. Now, here's the update that changes the game. Perplexity computer can now control Comet directly. So, the AI launches a browser agent inside Comet and that agent goes out and does whatever you asked. No APIs needed, no Zapia, no connectors, no integrations. It just opens the browser and does the job like a human would but faster. Hey, if we haven't met already, I'm the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of Goldie Agency. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every single comment. So, make sure you drop one below. Let's keep going. So, why is this such a huge deal? Here's the thing most people don't understand about AI agents. Up until now, if you wanted AI to do something on a website, that website needed an API. An API is basically a backdoor that lets software talk to other software. But most websites don't have good APIs. Some have none at all. So AI was stuck. It could only work with a handful of tools. This update blows that wide open. Now, the AI doesn't need an API. It just uses the website the same way you would. It sees the page. It clicks the buttons. It types in the fields. It navigates the menus. So that means any website that you can use, the AI can now use too. Let me give you some real examples so you can see how powerful this is. Example one, you run a business and you need leads. You tell the AI to find 10 companies in your niche, scrape their contact info, and draft outreach emails. The AI opens Google, searches for companies, visits their websites, pulls the info, writes the emails. Done. You didn't touch a thing. Example two, you need research. You tell it to find the top AI startups in healthcare and create a report. The AI opens articles, reads them, summarizes the key points, puts it all together in a clean report handed right to you. Example three, travel. You say, "Book the cheapest flight from New York to London next month." The AI opens travel sites, compares flights, finds the best option, and starts the booking process, all while you sit there doing nothing. Example four, content creation. You need a YouTube script about AI agents. You tell the AI, it goes out, researches the topic, gathers sources, summarizes everything, and writes a full script. That's wild. And this is just the beginning. Anything you can do in a browser, this AI can now do for you. Now, I need to tell you about something important. All right, let's keep going because there's more you need to know. This update puts perplexity in direct competition with some of the biggest names in AI. OpenAI has operator. Anthropic has claude computer use. There's auto GPT. There's Devon for coding. But here's what makes Perplexity different. Like they built it right into the browser. And that matters because where do you do most of your work? The browser, email, Google Docs, social media, project management tools, CRM, everything lives in the browser. So having an AI agent that controls the browser means it can reach basically everything. Now here's where it gets interesting and a little controversial. A US court recently stepped in and temporarily blocked Perplexity's AI browser agents from accessing Amazon accounts. The concern was about automated purchasing through logged in sessions. Basically, the AI was able to go on Amazon, use your account, and buy stuff. And Amazon said, "Hold on, that's not okay. This is a real legal battle happening right now." And it shows you two things. One, this technology is powerful enough that major companies are worried about it. And two, there are going to be rules and boundaries that get figured out as we go. But here's what I want you to take away from that. The fact that a court had to get involved tells you this is not a toy. This is real AI agents that can control browsers and use websites like humans are here. And they're only going to get better. Let me paint the bigger picture for you. We're watching a shift happen in real time. AI is going from a tool to a worker. Old AI answered your questions. New AI does your tasks. That's a massive difference. And this perplexity update is one of the clearest examples of that shift. Think about what this means for oneperson businesses. Think about what this means for small teams. You could have an AI agent doing your research, another one handling your outreach, another one managing your bookings, another one creating your content, all running inside a browser, all working at the same time. The internet is becoming an interface for AI agents, not just for humans. And the people who learn how to use these tools now are going to have a massive advantage because while everyone else is still doing things manually, you'll have AI doing the heavy lifting. Now, I want to be real with you. This is early. There are going to be bugs. There are going to be limits. The Amazon situation shows you that not every website is going to be cool with AI agents clicking around on their platform. But the direction is clear. AI agents that can use the internet like a human are the future. And Perplexity just took a big step toward making that real. So here's what I want you to do. First, go try a Comet. Download the browser. Play with it. See what it can do. The best way to learn this stuff is to get your hands on it. Second, start thinking about what tasks in your day could be automated with a browser agent. Emails, research, data entry, outreach, booking, reporting, make a list.
